title: Compute the I2C address byte with R/W bit
description: Combine a seven-bit device address with the read or write bit to form the byte actually placed on the bus.
body: Use when a datasheet lists a seven-bit address but the controller expects the full byte. Do not pass the seven-bit value where the eight-bit framed byte is required, since that misaddresses the device. Actions: shift the seven-bit address left by one, set the low bit for a read or clear it for a write, and verify the resulting byte against a bus capture. Validation signals include the addressed slave driving ACK and a capture whose address byte matches the computed value. Risk boundary: an off-by-one address can hit an unrelated device, so flag any address that responds unexpectedly before writing.
